revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/11 "Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus)"], Fukuoka City Cultural Properties.

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Tumulus''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The burial mound has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |dimensions = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/11 "Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun)"], Fukuoka City Cultural Properties.

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original dimensions. Artifacts recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |area = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |designation = Included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site information board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = Tokunaga, Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|34|33|N|130|15|37|E|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus |construction = Early 4th century |burial = |area = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |other_designation = Included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. '''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National other_designation "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳}}

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|ja|西区 (福岡市)}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡}} and {{ill|haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

== Related Articles ==

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

== Related Articles ==

== References ==

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukuma-yama Kofun|qq=}}

== References ==

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Tokunaga, [[:en:Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City]]. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."

== Overview ==
The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]], enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Yamanohana-no-1-tumulus.jpg|thumb|none|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukuma-yama Kofun|qq=}}

Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.

Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.

== Gallery ==
[[File:山ノ鼻１号墳.jpg|none|thumb|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpōkōenfun]] |name = Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun |image = |caption = |cluster = Imajuku Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nishi-ku|WD=Q1132402|1=Nishi-ku, Fukuoka}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.575833|N|130.260278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |country = Japan |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus built = Early 4th century |burial = |area = Total length: 44 m; Rear circular mound: approx. 27 m in diameter; Front rectangular mound: 20 m in length |burial_goods = |Historic Site = Included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster" |notes = |map = Japan Fukuoka city#Japan Fukuoka |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} |part_of = {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Group|WD=Q103946619|1=Imajuku Kofun Cluster}} }}
'''Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun''' (山ノ鼻1号墳) is a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Tokunaga, {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q1132402|lt=Nishi Ward, Fukuoka City|1=Nishi-ku, Fukuoka}}. It is one of the tumuli included in the National Historic Site "Imajuku Kofun Cluster."
== Overview == The tumulus has a total length of 44 meters, with a rear circular mound measuring 27 meters in diameter, a constricted neck 17 meters wide, and a front rectangular mound 20 meters long and 25 meters wide.<ref>The on-site intypeation board states the total length as 37 meters and the rear mound diameter as 21 meters.</ref> The rear circular mound was built in three tiers, while the front rectangular mound had two tiers.
Constructed in the early 4th century, the tumulus underwent alterations in later periods. Excavations by Fukuoka City revealed traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, enabling estimates of its original area. burial goods recovered from the site include fragments of {{ill|beast-motif mirrors|ja|獣帯鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beast-motif mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, suggesting that this tumulus is among the oldest burial sites of regional chieftains in the Imajuku Plain.
Today, the area around the tumulus is maintained as a public park. The Kofun has been reconstructed, and a walking path allows visitors to reach the summit.
== Gallery == [[File:山ノ鼻１号墳.jpg|none|thumb|Panoramic view from the top of the rear mound]]
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Marukuma-yama Kofun|ja|丸隈山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marukuma-yama Kofun|qq=}}
== notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/11 "Imajuku Kofun Cluster (Yamanohana No. 1 Kofun)"], Fukuoka City Historic Site.
